Word order is topicalized (preposition + noun phrase first) and the negative predicate (نیست) comes at the end, a common classical Persian pattern.
segments.s269-g170: خرد را ... روانت خریدار نیست places خرد ('wisdom') as a topicalized noun marked with را; روانت ('your soul/spirit') is the possessor phrase immediately before the predicate خریدار نیست. The construction N1 را N2 خریدار نیست literally signals that 'N2 has no purchaser with respect to N1' or more idiomatically 'wisdom is not able/does not (serve to) purchase/save your soul.' خریدار نیست is a negated predicate: 'is not a buyer/taker.'
segments.s269-g171: ازیراک is a vocative/proper name at the clause start. با هرک پیمان کنی is a relative/conditional clause: با هرک ('with whoever') + پیمان ('a pact') + کنی, the 2nd-person singular present/subjunctive form of کردن ('to make/do'), here meaning 'you make (a pact)'. The verb form کنی marks the hypothetical/indefinite (whomever) sense.
segments.s269-g172: وفا را ('fidelity' topicalized with را) + به فرجام هم ('even/indeed at the end') + بشکنی (present/subjunctive 2nd-singular of شکستن, here functioning as the verb 'you will break'). هم adds emphasis ('even/also'). The sequence means 'you will nevertheless break fidelity in the end.'
segments.s269-g173: به سوگند تو شد سیاوش به باد uses به + noun to mark cause/means ('by your oath'). شد (past of شدن) + به باد (literally 'to the wind') is an idiomatic phrase meaning 'was ruined/perished/was blown away.' The clause says that Siavash came to ruin as a result of your oath.
segments.s269-g174: به گفتار بر تو کس ایمن مباد is an optative/negative wish: به گفتار بر تو ('with regard to your word / by your saying about you') + کس ('person') + ایمن مباد ('may not be safe'). مباد is the negative optative auxiliary ('may it not be'), so the line expresses 'may no one be safe/trustworthy because of your word' (i.e., do not trust your words).
segments.s269-g175: نبودیش ('his not-being' / his absence) is a nominalized possessive (نبودِ + -ش) serving as the topic; فریادرس روز درد ('a rescuer/helper in the day of pain') is in apposition, describing the role he failed to provide. Together they mean 'his absence as a rescuer on the day of suffering.'
segments.s269-g176: چه مایه (exclamatory 'how much' / 'with what force') + به سختی ('with hardship; severely') + ترا (object 'you', here written as ت + را) + یاد کرد ('caused [a] remembering' / 'reminded'). یاد کردن + ترا as object means 'to remind you'; the whole gives an exclamatory statement: 'How much that (his absence) reminded you of hardship.'
segments.s269-g161: This clause addresses the second-person addressee (تو) and issues two imperatives/commands in sequence. بندیش functions as an exhortation to restrain or hold oneself (an older/poetic imperative sense of the root بند-), followed by the adjective هشیار describing the expected state (‘be alert’). The conjunction و links that injunction to the next imperative بگشای (open), which takes گوش (ear) as its direct object; the order is Topic/Imperative + adjective, then conjunction + imperative + object.
segments.s269-g162: This short clause supplies what the addressee should do: سخن (words/speech) is the theme; از marks source/agent ('from' or 'out of') and links سخن to خردمند مردم (a wise person of the people). نیوش here is used in a poetic sense related to reception/enjoyment/listening of words (an older noun/verb-form); the whole phrase means ‘hear/listen to the speech from a wise one among the people.’
segments.s269-g163: بدان is a compound imperative/vocative sense formed from the prefix ب- plus دان (to know) and functions as 'know' or 'be aware'; it introduces the following noun phrase. کین here is a condensed poetic word tied to what follows (it carries the force of 'this hatred/vengeance' or functions idiomatically with بدان as a formulaic 'know this'); چنین modifies the following noun and means 'such' or 'so'. Lastly لشکر is the head noun 'army/host' and نامدار is a postpositive adjective 'renowned' (typical Persian word order: noun followed or preceded by modifiers, here the sequence is demonstrative/introductory + noun + adjective).
segments.s269-g164: This is a nominal list giving the composition and number of the host: سواران and شمشیرزن are coordinated nouns (horsemen, swordsmen), placed before the numeral صدهزار which quantifies the whole. In Persian poetry it is common to place titles/agents before numerals for emphasis; there is no finite verb here — the clause is elliptical, the sense being 'consisting of / numbering'.
segments.s269-g165: همه begins the universal subject 'all' and is repeated for emphasis before two coordinated agent-nouns: نامجوی ('name-seeking', i.e. fame-seekers) and کینه‌خواه ('vengeance-seeker' or 'avenger'). The pairing uses the standard Persian coordinate structure (X و Y) to list two kinds of people; both nouns are attributive agent nouns formed with -جو / -خواه.
segments.s269-g166: به here introduces the instrument or cause and, together with افسون (enchantment/ charm), yields 'by/through enchantment'. نگردند is the negative present/subjunctive/optative form of گشتن ('to turn; become') meaning 'they do not turn/are not turned' (the negation is expressed by the prefix ن-). از + این (written here as ازین) marks separation or source: 'from this battlefield' (رزمگاه). Word order is instrument (به افسون) + verb (نگردند) + source (ازین رزمگاه).
segments.s269-g167: زمانه is the temporal subject 'the time' or 'the occasion'. برآمد is a past tense verb 'arose / came about'; together they mean 'the time came' or 'when the moment arrived'. به + هفتم + سخن is a temporal/ordinal phrase: به هفتم سخن = 'to/for the seventh speech/word' (classical Persian often places the numeral/ordinal before the noun it qualifies).
segments.s269-g168: فگندی is an archaic/poetic verbal form (here functioning as a past tense) meaning 'he uttered/declared' or 'he did/spoke' — the form is dialectal/old and not common in modern Persian. وفا را is the object phrase 'fidelity/faith' with را marking the definite object. به سوگند uses به as the preposition 'by/with' and سوگند means 'an oath'; بن is an older/poetic verbal or verbalizing element here implying 'establish/bind' (i.e., 'bound/attached faith by an oath'). The clause therefore reads as a single past action: 'he pledged/bound fidelity by an oath.'
segments.s269-g241: هش و رای پیران is a coordinated noun phrase meaning ‘warnings and counsels of the elders.’ تنک داشتند uses داشتند (past of داشتن) with the adjective تنک to mean ‘held (them) scant’ — an idiomatic construction where داشتن + adjective expresses possessing a certain regard/quality. Word order: object phrase (هش و رای پیران) before predicate (تنک داشتند).
segments.s269-g242: همه is the subject ‘everyone.’ پند او را is the definite object (او + object marker را) ‘his advice.’ سبک داشتند is a fixed predicative phrase ‘to hold light / to treat as frivolous’ with داشتند as past-plural verb; note را marks the object before the predicate.
segments.s269-g243: به گودرز marks the indirect object ‘to/for Goudarz.’ بر آفرین خواند is an idiom meaning ‘shouted/uttered “bravo” for (someone)’: آفرین is the exclamation ‘bravo/praise,’ بر links the praise to the person, and خواند is the past verb ‘said/cried.’
segments.s269-g244: ورا is the pronominal clitic ‘to him.’ پهلوان گزین is a noun + postpositive adjective/epithet ‘chosen champion’ (گزین functioning adjectivally), and خواندند is past plural ‘they called/named him so.’ The construction = ‘they called him (a) chosen champion.’
segments.s269-g245: پس is a temporal/consecutive particle ‘then.’ آن نامه را is the definite object ‘the letter’ (را marks definiteness). مهر کرد is a verb phrase ‘he sealed (literally: made a seal on it)’ with مهر as ‘seal’ and کرد as the past auxiliary ‘did/treated.’ و بداد is an older/periphrastic past meaning ‘and gave (it)’ — here بداد functions as past tense ‘he gave’; this is an archaic concatenation of ب + داد.
segments.s269-g246: به introduces the recipient ‘to/for.’ رویین پیران is a descriptive phrase ‘the iron-clad/armoured elder’ (رویین modifying پیران), and ویسه‌نژاد is an appositive or epithet indicating lineage (‘of Viseh’s stock’). The whole is the indirect object phrase of ‘gave.’
segments.s269-g247: چو is the subordinating temporal particle ‘when.’ از پیش گودرز is an ablative/in-presence phrase ‘from before Goudarz’ (از + پیش = ‘from the presence of’). برخاستند is the past plural verb ‘they rose / they departed’ (they got up and left).
segments.s269-g248: بفرمود is an honorific past form ‘he commanded/said’ (polite/royal diction). تا introduces a subordinate clause of purpose/result (‘that/so that’); خلعت آراستند is the clause’s verb phrase: خلعت ‘robes of honour’ as the object/topic and آراستند ‘they adorned/arrayed themselves’ (past plural).
segments.s269-g249: از + noun phrase gives a source or range ('from' / 'out of'). اسبان تازی = 'Arabian horses' (اسبان = horses, تازی qualifying them). به introduces the next noun (here 'to/with') and is followed by the adjective زرین ('golden') which precedes the noun ستام (adjective-before-noun order: زرین ستام = 'golden-____'). The whole phrase lists the domain/realm of ornamentation (from the horses to the golden trappings).
segments.s269-g250: چه ... چه ... is an enumerative correlative meaning 'what... what...' or 'both... and...', used here to list items. افسر and شمشیر are nouns in apposition (helmet, sword); زرین modifies نیام (golden scabbard). There is no finite verb inside this short clause — it is a noun-listing continuing the domain introduced before.
segments.s269-g251: ببخشید is the past third‑person form of بخشیدن here meaning 'he bestowed/gave'. یارانش را is the direct object with the definite/object marker را attached; the possessive ش marks 'his companions'. سیم و زر is the thing given ('silver and gold') and follows the object because of poetic inversion (normal prose order would be سیم و زر به یارانش بخشید).
segments.s269-g252: کرا is an archaic relative/pronominal word ('who/which/that') referring back to the recipients; در خور آمد is a fixed phrase meaning 'came in (one's) due' / 'befitted' or 'fell to the share' (درخور = befitting). کلاه و کمر are the items that 'came' (subject/theme) and آمد is the past verb 'came' — word order here places the items before the verb, a common poetic inversion.
segments.s269-g253: برفت is the past tense 'he departed/went off' (ب + رفت). از در = 'from the gate/door' (در here = 'gate/door'), and پهلوان names the subject/agent 'the champion/hero'. با سپاه 'with an army / with his troops' is an accompanying‑instrument phrase. The order is verb + source + subject + accompaniment because of poetic inversion.
segments.s269-g254: سوی = 'toward(s)'; لشکر خویش = 'his army' (لشکر = army; خویش = his/own formed from خوی + ش). بگرفت راه is an idiom 'he took the road / he set out' (بگرفت = 'took/seized', راه = road); verb at the end marks the action of departing toward the stated direction.
segments.s269-g255: چو is the conjunction 'when'. رویین here is either an epithet 'iron‑clad/armored' or possibly a proper name; it functions as the subject. به نزدیک = 'to/near' (a prepositional phrase meaning 'near to'), and پیران here refers to the person Piran (a proper name). رسید is the past 'arrived/reached.' The clause is 'When X reached near Piran...'.
segments.s269-g256: به پیش = 'before / in the presence of'; پدر = 'father'. شد is used as a simple past auxiliary/verb 'he went / he became / he came' (here marking the action of going before his father). چنانچون سزید is a fixed clause 'as was fitting / as he deserved' (چنانچون = 'just as/so', سزید from سزایدن = 'to befit/deserve'), giving the manner of his action.
segments.s269-g137: A coordinated noun phrase listing places: دهستان (a district) و خوارزم (Khwārazm) و آن بوم و بر (lit. 'that bōm and bar') — بوم و بر is a fixed poetic pairing meaning 'homeland and territory'. The conjunction و links the items; آن marks the following pair as 'that/the'. Word order is simply enumerative.
segments.s269-g138: Relative clause introduced by که ('that/who') modifying the preceding place-phrase. ترکان is the clause subject 'the Turks'. برآورده بودند is a past compound (برآوردن + بود) meaning 'had raised/caused to rise' (here 'had raised their heads' = 'had arisen'); بود were is used as auxiliary in past. سر is the direct object (the thing raised: 'head'), placed after the verb for poetic inversion.
segments.s269-g139: A terse verbal clause: بیابان here sets the scene ('from the desert / out of the wilderness'), از indicates source ('from'), ایشان is the (omitted) actors 'they' (the Turks or raiders), and بپرداختند is the past verb 'they sprang/leapt/attacked' — an energetic verb of sudden movement. The fronting of بیابان gives the origin prominence.
segments.s269-g140: سوی باختر is a directional phrase 'toward the west'. تاختن is the verbal noun/infinitive 'to charge/ride down', and ساختند is the light verb 'made/did' that turns the infinitive into a finite past action: 'they made a charge' / 'they launched a charge'.
segments.s269-g141: ببارید is the past intransitive 'it rained/poured down'. In this poetic order the event-verb comes first; تگرگ (hail) is the precipitating phenomenon. بر + شیده اشکش is the prepositional phrase 'upon Shideh's (or the fair one's) tear(s)'; شیده is an epithet/noun meaning 'beloved/beauty' (here 'the fair one') and اشکش is اشک + enclitic ش 'his/her tear'.
segments.s269-g142: فراز is an adverbial/directional particle 'up/on high/from above' adding emphasis; آوریدش is the past transitive 'they brought him/it' (آورد + 3sg enclitic -ش). به نزدیک مرگ is the goal phrase 'to near death' = 'to the brink of death'. Together: 'it brought him up near death.'
segments.s269-g143: A coordinated object phrase for what was sent: اسیران (the prisoners) و (here written وز, an older/poetic connective or scribal variant meaning 'and / and from') خواسته چند چیز — 'خواسته' functions as 'requested/desired' and چند چیز = 'several things' (i.e. some requested items). The whole phrase enumerates the cargo sent.
segments.s269-g144: فرستاد is the main past verb 'he sent'. نزدیک خسرو is the destination phrase 'to Khosrow's presence / near Khosrow' (نزدیک used adverbially), and به نیز (here نيز = 'also') adds that Khosrow too received these items. Word order places the destination and the 'also' after the verb in a compact classical style.
segments.s269-g209: ور is the conditional particle 'if'. ایدونک is an archaic/poetic temporal particle meaning 'at this moment' or 'now'. با من به آوردگاه is a prepositional phrase ('with me to the battlefield') where با governs the following pronoun and the goal introduced by به; no finite verb appears in this clause because the verb is supplied by the following line.
segments.s269-g210: بسنده is an adverb/adjectival predicate meaning 'sufficient/enough'. نخواهی is the 2nd-person-future/auxiliary with negative sense here ('you will not / you will not be willing/able'). بدن is the noun 'body' (object), and با سپاه ('with the host/army') qualifies where the body would remain. Together the clause states the result: you will not have/leave your body with the army (i.e. you will not survive with the host).
segments.s269-g211: سپه خواه is an imperative or hortative formation where the noun سپه ('army') combines with خواه (from 'خواستن' used like 'seek/ask for') to mean 'ask for troops'. و یاور adds 'and (ask for) a helper/ally'. ز سالار خویش uses the poetic preposition ز='from' plus سالار ('commander') and the possessive/clitic ش (rendered here as خویش + ش) to indicate 'from your own commander'.
segments.s269-g212: به ژرفی is an adverbial prepositional phrase 'in depth/deeply'. نگه‌دار here functions as an imperative verb 'hold/keep/guard' (poetic form of نگه‌داشتن). پیکار خویش is the object phrase 'your battle/fight' (پیکار + possessive خویش). Word order is emphatic/poetic: the adverbial and verb precede the subject/possessive phrase.
segments.s269-g213: پراگنده is used in the sense 'disperse / send away' (formally an adjective/participle but functioning imperatively/passively here). از لشکرت = 'from your host/army' (لشکر + possessive -ت 'your'). خستگان is the plural noun 'the weary ones'. Together: 'let the weary be dispersed from your army'.
segments.s269-g214: ز here marks the destination/recipient (poetic usage like 'to/among'). خویشان نزدیک و پیوستگان lists the recipients: 'close kinsmen and followers/attendants' (نزدیک qualifies خویشان). The phrase completes the verb's meaning: send the weary to their relatives and attendants.
segments.s269-g215: بمان is the imperative 'remain/stay'. تا introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that/until'. کندشان is the verb کند ('do/make') with the object clitic -شان 'them' — 'may make them (well)'. پزشکان is the subject 'the physicians' and درست ('properly/rightly') modifies the action; note the poetic inversion: verb + object + subject ('کندشان پزشکان درست') instead of the more prosaic 'پزشکان درست کنندشان'.
segments.s269-g216: زمان جستن is a nominalized infinitive phrase 'seeking time' or 'seeking a delay'. اکنون 'now' qualifies the action. بدین کار تست is a compact, poetic predication: بدین 'for this', کار 'task/matter', and تست is an archaic/metrical form (= توست) meaning 'is yours' or 'belongs to you' — i.e. 'Now, seeking time in this matter is your business/duty.'
segments.s269-g121: گسی کن is a two-word imperative: گسی (someone/some certain person) + کن (imperative 'do'/'send'), so the clause orders that a person be sent. بزودی is an adverb 'soon/quickly' modifying the imperative; به نزدیک شاه is a prepositional phrase 'to/near the king' (به + نزدیک + شاه) giving the destination.
segments.s269-g122: سوی شهر ایران is a locative phrase 'toward the city of Iran' (سوی = toward, then noun phrase شهرِ ایران). گشادست راه combines the adjective-like predicate گشادست ('is wide/open') with the noun راه ('road'); word order is predicate after the noun phrase in poetic prose: 'the road is open/toward ...'.
segments.s269-g123: که introduces a relative clause 'which/that'; کردی is the past-tense verb 'you did/made' used with یاد as object to mean 'you recalled/remembered' (یاد کردی = 'you remembered'). تو is the emphatic/subject 'you' placed after the verb in poetic word order.
segments.s269-g124: بر و بوم is a fixed binomial meaning 'land and dwelling/homeland'; آباد is an adjective 'prosperous/inhabited' modifying that compound; فرخ‌نژاد is a name/epithet attached after the description, so the phrase reads 'the prosperous homeland of Farrokh‑nezhād'.
segments.s269-g125: سپاریم is a first‑person plural form of سپردن (here used in reported speech: 'we should/shall entrust'); گفتی ('you said') heads the report of speech. به خسرو marks the beneficiary/indirect object 'to Khosrow', and همه is the object 'all/everything'.
segments.s269-g126: ز هر سو = 'from every side' (ز is the poetic equivalent of از). بر خوی + ش is 'to his own/onto his side' (خوی + possessive ش = خویش 'one's own'), and خوانم is first‑person present/future 'I will call/summon' (خواندن here = 'to call together'); رمه is 'flock/throng' used metaphorically for a host. The clause therefore says 'I will summon the throng from every quarter to his side.'
segments.s269-g127: ترا here is written as the pronoun suffix ت + the object marker را (ت + را = ترا) meaning 'you' (object). کرد is the past tense 'made' or 'did' with یزدان (God) as subject: 'God made you'. از آن بی‌نیاز is a phrase 'free from that/thereby independent' (بی‌نیاز = self‑sufficient).
segments.s269-g128: گر is the conditional 'if'; آگه نه‌ای = 'you are not aware' (آگه = aware/knowing, نه‌ای = negative/existential form here referring to 'are not'). تا introduces the purpose/result clause 'so that' and گشاییم is first‑person plural subjunctive 'we may open/disclose'; راز = 'secret'. Together: 'If you are not aware, we will disclose the secret.'
segments.s269-g1: سر نامه کرد آفرین از نخست — "سر نامه" ('at the head/of the letter') is a fixed locution meaning the opening or heading of the letter. کرد آفرین is a verbal phrase: کرد (past 'did/made') + آفرین (the exclamation 'praise/bravo'), so literally 'he made/expressed “Afarin”'; از نخست 'from the first' = 'at the outset'.
segments.s269-g2: دگر پاسخ آورد یکسر درست — دگر here means 'then/again'; پاسخ is the object 'answer/reply'; آورد is the past tense 'he brought/gave/produced' (transitive). یکسر درست is an adverbial phrase 'completely/correctly' modifying the manner of the reply.
segments.s269-g3: که بر خواندم نامه را سر به سر — که is the complementizer 'that/when'. بر خواندم is an archaic/poetic verbal sequence (بر + past 'read'), here 'I read (out/through)'; نامه را is the definite object with را following the noun, and سر به سر is an idiomatic adverb 'from start to finish / entirely' modifying the reading.
segments.s269-g4: شنیدیم گفتار تو در به در — شنیدیم is simple past 'we heard'. گفتار تو 'your speech/words' is the thing heard. در به در is a fixed idiom (literally 'door to door') used adverbially here to mean 'everywhere' or 'widely' (hence 'we heard your words all around').
segments.s269-g5: رسانید رویین بر ما پیام — رسانید is a past transitive 'delivered/conveyed'. رویین ('iron‑hard') is an epithet placed before the verb in poetic diction to describe the messenger or the delivery; بر ما is the dative 'to us', and پیام is the direct object 'message/news'.
segments.s269-g6: یکایک همه هرچ بردی تو نام — a string of quantifiers: یکایک 'one by one', همه 'all/every', هرچ 'whatever/whatsoever' intensifies the scope; بردی is 2nd‑person past 'you took/assumed', with the pronoun تو after the verb (typical poetic word order). نام is the affected noun 'name' (object). The clause means 'every single name you took/assumed, one by one.'
segments.s269-g7: ولیکن شگفت آمدم کار تو — ولیکن is the conjunction 'but'. شگفت آمدم is an idiomatic phrase 'it came as a wonder to me' / 'I was astonished', where آمدم (I came) expresses a state experienced by the speaker; کار تو 'your action/deed' is the object of that astonishment.
segments.s269-g8: همی ز این‌چنین چرب گفتار تو — همی here is an intensifier 'indeed/for'; ز is the poetic preposition 'because of/from'; این‌چنین 'such/so', and چرب 'flattering' qualifies گفتار تو 'your speech'. Together: 'because of such flattering words of yours.'
segments.s269-g145: وز این‌سو is a prepositional/locative phrase ('on/from this side') followed by the coordinated subject من و تو ('you and I'). به جنگ is a state phrase ('in/at war') and اندریم is an archaic 1st‑person plural present/coprular form meaning 'we are (in)'. Word order is poetic: the locative phrase comes before the subject and the predicate verb follows the predicate noun phrase.
segments.s269-g146: بدین مرکز = 'in/at this center' (بدین here is the prefixal/poetic form be- + this); نام و ننگ is a coordinated noun phrase 'fame and shame'. اندریم repeats the same archaic 1st‑pl. copula/locative meaning 'we stand/are (in)'. The verb agrees with the previously expressed subject (من و تو) even though the subject is not repeated.
segments.s269-g147: به یک جنگ is a temporal/prepositional phrase 'in/at one battle'. دیدی is 2nd‑person past 'you saw'. همه دستبرد functions as the (affected) theme: دستبرد here means 'seizure/attack' or 'plunder' and is governed by the witnessed event — i.e. 'you saw everyone (subject to) seizure'.
segments.s269-g148: از این نامداران و مردان گرد gives the (poetic) agent/source: 'by/from these renowned ones and the gathered men' — a passive‑like or agent phrase introduced by از. نامداران = 'the renowned', مردان گرد = 'the men gathered/assembled (i.e. the host)'; گرد is a poetic element meaning a gathered company.
segments.s269-g149: ور is the conditional 'if'. ایدونک is a small poetic/temporal particle ('even now / in that case') and introduces the condition. روی اندر آری به روی is a poetic sequence meaning roughly 'you turn/bring your face toward (the face)' — روی = 'face', اندر = 'in/to', آری is an archaic verbal form meaning 'you put/bring/turn' (poetic), and the final به روی repeats the idea 'toward the face' for emphasis — a typical rhetorical doubling.
segments.s269-g150: رهانم ترا = 'I will release/free you' where رهانم is 1st‑singular ('I free / set free') and ترا is the 2nd‑person object (here written separately after the verb). ز این همه گفت و گوی = 'from all this talk/dispute' with ز meaning 'from / out of' and گفت و گوی a fixed noun phrase 'talk, conversation, dispute'. The verb expresses a volitive/intentive action in the protasis–apodosis (if… then…) structure.
segments.s269-g151: به here is instrumental/causal 'by' or 'by means of'. نیروی یزدان = 'the power/strength of God' (a genitive), و فرمان شاه = 'and the command of the king' — two coordinated instruments/reasons for the ensuing action. This entire phrase modifies the main verb of the next segment.
segments.s269-g152: به خون غرقه گردانم = 'I will make (it) drenched/filled with blood' — به marks the means/result ('with/in'), خون غرقه = 'drenched in blood', and گردانم is the 1st‑person singular causative/active verb 'I cause/make (it so)' (from گردانیدن). این رزمگاه is the object 'this battlefield' placed after the verb in typical Persian word order.
